questions.question: Which of the following quantities remains unchanged when a charged particle moves in a magnetic field?
Which technology uses acoustic waves to locate objects in the ocean?
Who, in 1816, modified Newton's formula for the speed of sound to take into account adiabatic compression in air?
Which of the following is a preventive measure for nuclear disasters?
questions.option_1: Comprehensive monitoring and regular testing to detect equipment or operator failures
questions.option_2: Ignoring minor radiation leaks to avoid panic
questions.option_3: Constructing nuclear plants near residential areas

questions.question: If an object moves up to four times faster than the speed of sound, the condition is called:
Which renewable energy source is dependent on the gravitational pull of the moon?
Which of the following is an example of a longitudinal wave?
What is the phenomenon called when a wire passes through a block of ice without splitting it and the water refreezes after the wire passes?
Impulse is defined as _________.
In which year did Andre-Marie Ampère publish his magnum opus ‘Mathematical Theory of Electrodynamic Phenomena Deduced Solely from Experiments’?
What is the dimensional formula for mechanical energy that refers to the total energy of an object due to its position and motion?
If the temperature of air increases, what happens to the speed of sound and the minimum distance to hear an echo?
Who is credited with the discovery of the nucleus through the gold foil experiment?
Which of the following is a dimensionless quantity?
Which of the following statements about friction is correct?
questions.option_1: Static friction opposes actual motion, while kinetic friction opposes impending motion.
questions.option_2: Kinetic friction is always greater than static friction.
questions.option_3: Static friction opposes impending motion and kinetic friction opposes actual motion.
questions.option_4: Both static and kinetic friction depend on the area of contact.

questions.question: Which of the following power projects is based on nuclear energy?
In 1995, physicists Eric Cornell and Carl Wieman created the first Bose-Einstein condensate (BEC) using which isotope?
What can be the shape of the trajectory of a charged particle moving in a uniform magnetic field?
What is the value of one curie (Ci) in SI units of radioactivity?
Who first proposed a law in 1931 to clarify the idea of thermal equilibrium and its significance in determining temperature?
An infrasound microphone enables the remote detection of aircraft wake vortices, clear air turbulence, tornadoes, and seismic events at frequencies below:
Who is best known for being associated with the theory of quantum mechanics, published in 1925?
Which one of the following hypotheses/theories explains the formation of the solar system from a rotating cloud of gas and dust?
Which of the following can have higher optical density but lower mass density?
What is the name of the principle which states that each point on a wavefront acts as a source of secondary wavelets that spread in all directions and form a new wavefront?
